Documentation.clue: After a pitot-static certification, this document returns the aircraft to service.
choices: Aircraft logbook entry with test data and approval
An FAA Form 337 Major Alteration
A Service Bulletin compliance letter
An Airworthiness Directive status report
Documentation.explanation: A logbook entry returns the aircraft to service.

Documentation.clue: This document is required when a VHF COM radio is swapped for an identical part number unit.
choices: An FAA Form 337 for major alteration
Logbook entry for maintenance performed
STC approval from the radio manufacturer
FSDO approval required before flight
Documentation.explanation: Identical-PN swap uses a logbook entry.

Documentation.clue: This is the condition that requires an FAA Form 337 for avionics work.
choices: Any radio replacement regardless of type
All work performed under Part 145
Major alterations to aircraft type design
Any component repair returned to service
Documentation.explanation: Form 337 is triggered by major alterations.

Documentation.clue: This person signs the aircraft logbook returning an aircraft to service after completing an STC autopilot installation.
choices: The STC holder company representative
Only the IA who supervised the work
The Part 145 repair station manager
The technician who performed the work
Documentation.explanation: The certifying technician signs the logbook RTS.

Documentation.clue: This is the correct work-order approach when several LRUs were replaced during troubleshooting before the real fault was found.
choices: List all components replaced and troubleshooting steps
Document only the final repair that fixed the problem
Note that warranty may be void for excessive swapping
Request engineering review before completing the documentation
